title: Market Brief — Monday, May 18, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit in oversupply at 85% availability, H100s are loose at 70%, and MI300X holds at 60%, but B200s are scarce at 25% and GB200s are in severe shortage at just 10% availability. The biggest price signal is H200 OCPI at $7.01/hr versus a $3.00 spot median, a 134% premium on trade-based pricing with 121.7% annualized volatility, screaming that next-gen capacity is being hoarded.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit in oversupply at 85% availability, H100s are loose at 70%, and MI300X holds at 60%, but B200s are scarce at 25% and GB200s are in severe shortage at just 10% availability. The biggest price signal is H200 OCPI at $7.01/hr versus a $3.00 spot median, a 134% premium on trade-based pricing with 121.7% annualized volatility, screaming that next-gen capacity is being hoarded.

On Llama 70B reference, every provider selling at medium tier on A100/H100/MI300X is burning cash, with MI300X losses hitting $12.90 per million tokens after TCO adjustment versus a $3.00 medium-tier price. DeepSeek: Burning Cash, pricing V3.2 output at $0.38/M against a TCO-adjusted production cost near $5.03/M on H100. OpenAI: Neutral, hedged across premium tiers. Anthropic: Strong on Opus margins. Google: Weak, underpricing Gemini 2.5 Flash at $2.50 output against thin margins.

B200 inference is undervalued: at $4.02/M raw cost it is the only GPU delivering positive margins at the high tier, and spot availability at 25% means pricing has room to run. H100 spot at $2.00/hr is overvalued relative to its $7.87/M inference cost, a capacity trap for anyone not serving premium-tier traffic.
bullets: 25% B200 availability signals compute scarcity in the only GPU class with positive high-tier inference margins.
10% GB200 availability marks a severe shortage with no reliable spot pricing yet established.
$7.01/hr H200 OCPI represents a 134% premium over the $3.00 spot median, the widest gap across all GPU classes.
90.3% H100 utilization surged 10.5 percentage points in 30 days despite 70% nominal availability, indicating shadow demand.
$0.06/M output for Meta-Llama-3.1-8B marks the floor, a 99.9% decline from GPT-4 launch pricing.
21.76 LLMflation index means frontier output costs have fallen roughly 78% from the March 2023 baseline.
